UK Personal Tax Services

Self Assessment Tax Returns

Personal tax returns for directors, freelancers, and high-net-worth individuals. Our tax specialists ensure accurate calculations, timely submissions, and legitimate tax planning.

Get a QuoteBook Consultation

Who Needs to File Self Assessment?

Company Directors

All directors of UK companies must file personal tax returns regardless of whether they draw a salary.

Self-Employed & Freelancers

Individuals with self-employment income over £1,000 must file annual self assessment returns.

High Income Individuals

Those with income over £100,000, capital gains, or foreign income must file self assessment.

Property Income

Landlords receiving rental income must file returns, even if the property makes a loss.

Trustees & Executors

Those responsible for trust estates or deceased estates must file tax returns on behalf of beneficiaries.

Foreign Income Earners

UK residents with foreign income or gains must declare these on their self assessment return.

Our Self Assessment Services

Tax Return Preparation

Complete preparation of your self assessment tax return with accurate calculations of all income sources and deductions.

Tax Planning

Proactive tax planning throughout the year to optimize your position and minimize tax liabilities legally.

Capital Gains Tax

Calculation and reporting of capital gains on property sales, investments, and business disposals.

Payment on Account

Management of HMRC payment on account requirements and cash flow planning for tax payments.

Tax Investigation Support

Full support during HMRC enquiries and investigations including representation and documentation.

Non-Resident Tax

Specialist advice for non-residents with UK income or UK residents with foreign income.

Current Income Tax Rates (2024/25)

Tax BandIncome RangeRate
Personal AllowanceUp to £12,5700%
Basic Rate£12,571 - £50,27020%
Higher Rate£50,271 - £125,14040%
Additional RateOver £125,14045%

*Personal allowance reduces by £1 for every £2 earned above £100,000. Rates subject to change.

Self Assessment Timeline

01

Information Gathering

We request all necessary tax documents and income information.

02

Tax Calculation

We calculate your tax position including all reliefs and allowances.

03

Review & Approval

We review the return with you and obtain your approval.

04

Filing & Payment

We file with HMRC and manage payment on account requirements.

Important Deadlines: Paper returns due 31 October. Online returns due 31 January. Tax payment due 31 January. We track all deadlines and ensure timely submission.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the deadline for self assessment?

The deadline for online submission is 31 January following the end of the tax year (5 April). Paper returns must be submitted by 31 October. Tax payments are due by 31 January.

What are payment on account?

If your tax bill is over £1,000, you must make advance payments towards your next year's tax. These are due by 31 January and 31 July, each representing 50% of your previous year's liability.

What records do I need to keep?

You must keep records for at least 22 months from the filing deadline (5 years and 10 months for self-employed). This includes income records, expense receipts, bank statements, and investment documentation.

Can you help with late returns?

Yes. We can prepare and file late returns and help negotiate with HMRC regarding penalties. We also provide support during tax investigations.

Simplify Your Tax Return

Let our experts handle your self assessment while you focus on what matters. Book a free consultation.
